meanings.usage: 修饰动物或人，指失去自由、被关起来的状态
指被关押或圈养的对象
用于按顺序对应前面提到的两个或多个事物
用来描述人或动物因受不公对待而产生怨恨情绪的状态
常用结构assign sb. to sth./to do sth.，表示分派某人做某项工作或负责某个对象
特指以训练、看管大象为职业的人
不可数名词，指对人或事物的喜爱、眷恋之情，常与for/to搭配
常用搭配pine for sb./sth.表示思念，pine away表示日渐消瘦
固定搭配deprive sb. of sth.，表示夺去某人原本拥有的东西
引申义为突出某个事实或观点的重要性，相当于emphasize
常与to搭配，指愿意服从他人命令的状态
指动物或人突然用力地向前冲、扭动身体的动作
特指用绳索把动物拴在某个固定物体上
形容问题或任务需要小心处理，稍有不慎就会出问题
形容声音、动作等能让人或动物情绪平静下来
常用复数形式，指为了让对方听从自己而说的好听的话

phrases.usage: 介词短语，可表示“除了……之外（还）”或“除了……之外（都不）”，相当于besides或except for

examples.en: Apart from any moral considerations this is a stupid method of training.
examples.zh: 且不说道德方面的考虑，这种训练方法本身就很愚蠢。

examples.en: Apart from English, he also learns French and Japanese.
examples.zh: 除了英语之外，他还学习法语和日语。

phrases.phrase: take sth. with a grain of salt
phrases.usage: 固定习语，表示对某事半信半疑，不完全相信

examples.en: Such extreme cases must probably be taken with a grain of salt.
examples.zh: 这类极端案例可能不能完全当真。

examples.en: You should take what he says with a grain of salt, he often exaggerates.
examples.zh: 他说的话你得打个折扣听，他经常夸张。

phrases.usage: 习语，意为“挣钱维持生计；值得雇佣”，指通过劳动抵消自己的生活成本

examples.en: It is then almost ready to undertake heavy work and can begin to earn its keep straight away.
examples.zh: 这个年龄的大象差不多已经可以干重活，能立刻开始干活抵偿饲养成本了。

examples.en: Even as a teenager, he did part-time jobs to earn his keep instead of relying on his parents.
examples.zh: 甚至十几岁的时候，他就靠打零工养活自己，不依赖父母。

phrases.usage: 被动结构，表示“被剥夺……；失去……”，后接被夺走的事物

examples.en: They have been deprived of their own trainer.
examples.zh: 他们被夺走了自己的驯象师。

examples.en: Many children in remote areas are deprived of the right to receive education.
examples.zh: 很多偏远地区的孩子被剥夺了受教育的权利。

phrases.usage: 介词短语，表示“在……的伴奏下；伴随着……

examples.en: Others rub their hands over his skin to the accompaniment of a monotonous and soothing chant.
examples.zh: 其他人一边摸着它的皮肤，一边唱着单调的、让人安心的歌谣。

examples.en: The performance began to the accompaniment of warm applause.
examples.zh: 演出在热烈的掌声中拉开了序幕。

phrases.usage: 固定搭配，表示“易受……影响的；对……敏感的”，后接名词或动名词

examples.en: The elephant is not immediately susceptible to such blandishments.
examples.zh: 大象并不会立刻就吃这套哄骗的把戏。

examples.en: Babies are susceptible to colds when the weather changes suddenly.
examples.zh: 天气骤变的时候，婴儿很容易感冒。

phrases.usage: 动词短语，意为“由……组成”，不用于被动语态，主语是整体，宾语是组成部分

examples.en: The former method simply consists of setting an elephant to work and beating him until he does what is expected of him.
examples.zh: 前一种方法无非就是强迫大象干活，打它，直到它按要求做事为止。

examples.en: The research team consists of three professors and five graduate students.
examples.zh: 这个研究团队由三名教授和五名研究生组成。

phrases.usage: 固定搭配，表示“有能力做……；能够做出……”，后接名词或动名词

examples.en: Elephants are capable of a considerable degree of personal affection.
examples.zh: 大象能产生相当深厚的个人感情。

examples.en: This new device is capable of detecting even tiny leaks in the pipe.
examples.zh: 这个新设备甚至能检测到管道里极其微小的泄漏。

grammar.title: 非限制性定语从句
grammar.definition: 对主句中的某个名词或整个主句内容进行补充说明，去掉之后不影响主句的完整意思，通常和主句之间用逗号隔开
grammar.usage: 用来补充额外信息，which可指代前面的整个句子或句子中的某个名词，who指代人

examples.en: Two main techniques have been used for training elephants, which we may call respectively the tough and the gentle.
examples.zh: 训练大象主要有两种方法，我们可以分别称之为强硬法和温柔法。

examples.en: The gentle method requires more patience in the early stages, which produces a cheerful good-tempered elephant.
examples.zh: 温柔法在初期需要更多耐心，这种方法能训练出性格开朗温顺、能多年忠诚服役的大象。

grammar.title: 原因状语从句
grammar.definition: 用来解释主句动作或情况发生的原因，常用引导词有for, because, since, as等
grammar.usage: for引导的原因状语从句通常不放在句首，是对前面内容的补充说明，语气比because弱

examples.en: Apart from any moral considerations this is a stupid method of training, for it produces a resentful animal who at a later stage may well turn man-killer.
examples.zh: 且不说道德方面的考虑，这种训练方法本身就很愚蠢，因为它会让动物产生怨恨，之后很可能会变成伤人的野兽。

examples.en: The most economical age to capture an elephant for training is between 15 and 20 years, for it is then almost ready to undertake heavy work.
examples.zh: 捕捉大象进行训练最划算的年龄是15到20岁之间，因为这个年龄的大象差不多已经可以干重活了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Two main ... have been used for ..., which we may call respectively A and B.

original.en: Two main techniques have been used for training elephants, which we may call respectively the tough and the gentle.
original.zh: 训练大象主要有两种方法，我们可以分别称之为强硬法和温柔法。

imitations.en: Two main methods have been used for learning foreign languages, which we may call respectively the rote-learning method and the immersion method.
imitations.zh: 学习外语主要有两种方法，我们可以分别称之为死记硬背法和沉浸法。

imitations.en: Two main solutions have been proposed for this environmental problem, which we may call respectively the technological approach and the policy approach.
imitations.zh: 针对这个环境问题，人们提出了两种主要解决方案，我们可以分别称之为技术方案和政策方案。

sentencePatterns.pattern: The first essential in ... is to ... who will be responsible for ...

original.en: The first essential in elephant training is to assign to the animal a single mahout who will be entirely responsible for the job.
original.zh: 训练大象的第一要务是给大象指派一名专门的驯象师，由他全权负责这项工作。

imitations.en: The first essential in project management is to appoint a project manager who will be entirely responsible for the whole process.
imitations.zh: 项目管理的第一要务是任命一名项目经理，由他全权负责整个流程。

imitations.en: The first essential in dog training is to let the dog recognize a sole owner who will be responsible for its daily care and instruction.
imitations.zh: 训练狗狗的第一要务是让狗狗认可唯一的主人，由主人负责它的日常照顾和指令训练。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Such extreme cases must probably be taken with a grain of salt, but they do underline the general principle that ...

original.en: Such extreme cases must probably be taken with a grain of salt, but they do underline the general principle that the relationship between elephant and mahout is the key to successful training.
original.zh: 这类极端案例可能不能完全当真，但它们确实突出了一个普遍原则：大象和驯象师之间的关系是训练成功的关键。

imitations.en: Such unbelievable success stories must probably be taken with a grain of salt, but they do underline the general principle that persistent effort is the foundation of achievement.
imitations.zh: 这类令人难以置信的成功故事可能不能完全当真，但它们确实突出了一个普遍原则：持续的努力是成就的基础。

imitations.en: Such rare medical cases must probably be taken with a grain of salt, but they do underline the general principle that early detection of disease greatly improves treatment effects.
imitations.zh: 这类罕见的医疗案例可能不能完全当真，但它们确实突出了一个普遍原则：疾病的早期发现能极大提升治疗效果。
